programmes.show_description: 60 minutes of hits and headlines from the year of 'Moonraker', 'Alien' and 'The Muppet Movie', and when 24 million people saw Penelope Keith in 'To The Manor Born' on TV.
Seventies music power to play loud! Work out your speakers with some pure power pop and rock including AC/DC, Kiss and Meat Loaf!

Tracks with big TV connections! Could be theme tunes, adverts or just a song made famous by a TV show.

Another batch of silly songs, dodgy videos & novelty hits from the 70s. Yes every one of these was a huge hit!

They all had 1 massive song, then never skirted the UK Top 20 singles chart again! Here are 70 acts from every year of the 70s, in order, who all achieved their 15 minutes of fame.
Two hours of disco babes who ruled the dancefloors! From Labelle and Lipps Inc to Tina Charles and Sister Sledge - it's all girl singers, all the way!
Songs you really like but are a bit too embarrassed to admit. Don't worry, we're all in the same boat here & Pat urges you to try to have a little less shame in your musical game!

programmes.show_name: Help Me Make It Through the Night!
programmes.show_description: Non-stop love songs, back to back all night, from the era of Leo Sayer, Carpenters, The Bee Gees & Minnie Ripperton.

programmes.show_description: From a 'Horse With No Name' to a 'Dog Named Boo' - 12 songs about animals, or by artists with animal connections. Pretty contrived but quite good fun.

They say music goes in cycles! Well for a period in the 70s we decided to celebrate all things 50s - go figure! From Racey, Mud, Darts and Showaddywaddy to the hit movie Grease.

programmes.show_name: Bruno Brookes UK Top 40 of the 70s
programmes.show_description: Including ten big million-sellers back when it was a rare achievement! These are 40 of the biggest-selling songs of the decade with Bay City Rollers, ABBA, Queen, Blondie and Wings.

Get on the Love train with some top tunes to satisfy those Boogie Nights!
From the early days when soul gained that disco beat in 1973, to the turn of the decade when Blondie and Diana Ross got in on the act! Simon Bates is your dancefloor host!
